goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) makes isolation feel like physical pain right now. I need constant social validation and proximity to diffuse my stress about the essay. My avoidant nature prevents me from sitting with my own thoughts, so I must be near someone else.

goals.description: Dive immediately into the casual game stream/chat for at least another hour, actively ignoring the scholarship application until the stress becomes physically painful.
goals.rationale: I am impulsive with a short planning horizon. The immediate dopamine from the game and social chat is real, while the deadline consequence feels abstract and far away. Pushing the work back reduces current friction, which I prioritize over future consequences.

goals.description: Maintain strict silence about the scholarship status; if asked, deflect with vague excuses or change the subject to avoid admitting I'm struggling or behind.
goals.rationale: My conflict-avoidant style means I will do anything to prevent a direct conversation about my failure or procrastination. Admitting I'm behind creates interpersonal friction that I cannot tolerate right now.
